Title: Transgenic Cotton Plants Expressing Double-stranded RNAs Target HMG-CoA Reductase (HMGR) Gene Inhibits the Growth, Development and Survival of Cotton Bollworms

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA to mevalonate in the mevalonate pathway, supplying precursor for juvenile hormone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Insect larval metamorphosis, growth, development and reproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Plant-delivered dsRNA targeting HMGR suppresses HMGR transcript and protein in Helicoverpa armigera larvae, impairing growth, development and survival and indirectly reducing vitellogenin expression.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transgenic cotton expressing dsRNA against HMGR significantly reduced endogenous HMGR transcript levels in larvae, with up to 80.68% reduction in line HMGi1-7. 2. HMGR protein accumulation in larvae decreased, reaching 35.71% lower than control after 144 h feeding. 3. HMGR silencing caused higher larval mortality, reduced net weight gain, smaller body size, and abnormal soft/thin pupal cuticle. 4. Silencing HMGR also reduced vitellogenin transcript levels by up to 76.86%, linking HMGR-dependent metabolism to reproductive capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic plant-mediated RNAi; PCR; Southern blot; RT-PCR/qRT-PCR of dsRNA in cotton; qRT-PCR of larval HMGR; ELISA for HMGR protein; insect feeding bioassays measuring mortality, body size, net weight gain and pupal phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as a rate-limiting enzyme in the mevalonate pathway of juvenile hormone synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in transgenic cotton leaves as dsRNA construct expression; higher in selected T1 lines.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Larvae fed on transgenic cotton leaves expressing dsHMGR; effects assessed over 48-168 h.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transgenic cotton plants themselves exhibited normal growth and phenotype; insecticidal effect was not immediately lethal to all larvae and depended on larval stage and dsRNA target fragment/accumulation level.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an RNAi pest-resistance target to pyramid with Bt cotton for multiple pest resistance and delay resistance evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study investigated insect HMGR as the causal target gene, while the applied species was transgenic cotton producing dsRNA rather than expressing the insect enzyme for metabolism engineering. HMGi2 fragment may be more effective than HMGi1 for lethality despite lower dsRNA expression.
